title: Stress reaction
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed suicide, self-harm, bipolar, psychotic, substance, mental-status, function, support, and risk fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations

red_flags.text: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-confirmed suicide, self-harm, severe-function, and safety warning prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

assessment.text: Describes depressed mood in response to an identifiable psychosocial stressor and supports documenting single, multiple, recurrent, or continuing social and occupational stressors.
assessment.rationale: Supports stressor and adjustment terminology plus event, situational, social, and occupational context without making a diagnosis.
assessment.evidence_extract: Describes depressed mood in response to an identifiable psychosocial stressor and supports documenting single, multiple, recurrent, or continuing social and occupational stressors.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Differential diagnosis — adjustment disorder with depressed mood

medication.text: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.
medication.rationale: Supports anxiety, low mood, sleep, concentration, function, medical or medication history, stressor, and support context.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Sections 5–7 — depressive features and structured assessment

medication.text: Defines acute traumatic stress through intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, and significant functional impairment after a potentially traumatic event and supports clinician-led psychological-first-aid or trauma-focused care documentation without medication invention.
medication.rationale: Supports acute traumatic-stress terminology and trauma-exposure context only when a potentially traumatic event and relevant symptoms are present.
medication.evidence_extract: Defines acute traumatic stress through intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, and significant functional impairment after a potentially traumatic event and supports clinician-led psychological-first-aid or trauma-focused care documentation without medication invention.

source.title: Guidelines for the management of conditions specifically related to stress

exact_section.heading: Acute traumatic stress symptoms in the first month after a potentially traumatic event

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, safety planning, questions, and arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

source_limitations: Panic symptoms, dissociation, work or school-note issuance, fixed one-to-two-week review, broad duration presets, and the combined multi-section history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer adjustment disorder, acute stress disorder, depression, PTSD, treatment, medicine, therapy referral, sick leave, or risk level.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
